title: Adding Angles That Share a Vertex

steps.instruction: If two angles share a side and a vertex (no overlap), what can we say about the total?
steps.work: The measure of the whole angle equals the sum of the parts: total = angle 1 + angle 2.
steps.explanation: This is the angle addition postulate — angles add up just like lengths do.

steps.instruction: A ray splits a 90° angle into two parts: 35° and what?
steps.explanation: Since the parts must add up to the whole, we subtract the known part.
The two parts add back to the original angle.

title: Finding a Missing Angle on a Straight Line

steps.instruction: What do angles on a straight line sum to?
steps.work: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°.
steps.explanation: A straight line is a straight angle = 180°.

steps.instruction: Three angles on a line are 45°, 85°, and x°. Find x.
steps.explanation: All angles on the line must add to 180°.

steps.instruction: Add known angles and subtract from 180°.

steps.explanation: Confirmed: all angles on the line add to a straight angle.
